description_long: This guard stone is a robust, weathered stone structure composed of two stacked rectangular stone blocks forming a sturdy base, topped by a roughly hewn, upright stone with a rounded peak. The base stones display significant textural variation, with visible cracks, chips, and areas of moss or lichen growth, suggesting long-term outdoor exposure. The upper stone is irregularly shaped, with a slanted top and smoother, worn surfaces, likely from years of environmental wear. On the front face of the upper stone, there is a faintly inscribed rectangular plaque area, possibly bearing a name or date, though the text is largely eroded. The coloration is predominantly gray with subtle variations, including patches of white and hints of green from organic growth. The overall form and massiveness of the object indicate its function as a protective barrier, likely to prevent vehicle encroachment onto pedestrian areas or property.
description: A weathered, multi-tiered stone guard stone with a rough, upright top and a sturdy, cracked base, featuring faint inscriptions and patches of moss. Its robust, aged appearance suggests use as a protective roadside barrier.
description_view_0: Front view shows the inscribed plaque area on the upper stone and the layered, cracked base with some moss growth.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the irregular, rounded shape of the upper stone and the rough, textured surfaces of the base blocks.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back of the upright stone, which is smoother and less detailed, and the layered structure of the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the thickness of the base stones and the slanted, worn surface of the upper stone.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the edge of urban streets, near the corners of sidewalks, driveways, or entrances to historical sites, often adjacent to pedestrian pathways or building facades to prevent vehicle intrusion.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, with the base resting flush on a paved or stone surface, and the upright stone positioned vertically.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and erosion on the edges and corners of the base stones, with surface smoothing and discoloration on the upper stone due to prolonged weather exposure. Moss and lichen growth are present in crevices, likely from moisture retention. The front face shows more wear, possibly from occasional vehicle contact.
attribute_historical_markings: A faint, rectangular inscribed plaque area is visible on the front face of the upper stone, with most text eroded and illegible.
